variance_note: Re-recorded 2026-07-22 at commit eb85484: host steady-state now includes resident AI services (SwarmUI/ComfyUI) vs the 2026-07-18 recording; the 2026-07-21 differential proved the drift environmental (pre-change build failed the old band identically, measured at 16.1 landing). Two consecutive runs on alder. raf_duration p99 stable (1.3 vs 1.3); input-latency p50 stable (9.5 vs 9.8); p99 varies (44.5 vs 50.6, small n).

palette.note: WebGL2: 256×1 RGB texSubImage2D when paletteDirty. n=2 means palette changed only at startup; stays constant during E1M1. Sub-0.1 ms — negligible. Confirms 12.2a aggregate verdict.

upload.note: WebGL2: 320×200 R8 texSubImage2D + drawArrays. p99 = 0.2 ms, max = 0.3 ms. Negligible at 35 Hz budget (28.6 ms/frame). Confirms 12.2a aggregate verdict.

raf_interval.note: Frame-to-frame rAF timestamp delta (ms). p50/p90/p99 ≈ 16.7 ms (ideal 60 Hz = 16.667 ms) — extremely tight. Max 50–67 ms is 1–3 dropped frames (OS jitter spike). In headless Chrome rAF is not vsync-locked; interval reflects CPU scheduling fidelity, not display timing.

raf_duration.note: Total rAF callback duration: input.frame() + doom._web_frame() + renderer.draw(). p50=0.2 ms, p99=0.8–0.9 ms. Well within 16.7 ms frame budget.

worklet.note: AudioWorklet process() timing unavailable in headless Chrome. worklet instrumentation is functional (port message protocol implemented in music-worklet.js + audio.js); requires interactive session or --autoplay-policy override that allows AudioContext activation. See headless-limitation note in perf.md.

input_latency.note: keydown event.timeStamp → renderer.draw() returns. p50 ≈ 8–9 ms (≈ 0.5 × 16.7 ms frame interval, as expected for random-phase event landing mid-frame). p90 ≈ 15–17 ms (≈ 1 frame). p99 varies (35–61 ms) due to small n and occasional OS scheduling spikes. Measurement scope: event firing → GPU upload call (compositing not waited). Tic-exact attribution not feasible without engine changes.

canvas_info.note: task 18.3: DOM canvas dimensions and renderer kind. Not a timing stage; no run1/run2; browser-pipeline-compare.mjs SKIPS this stage. 320×200 = wideMode off (default). 854×200 when wide enabled.
